blockingIssues.description: Line 125: ExtractionConfig::default() sets store_path to '.skills' but AGENTS.md specifies user-extensible skills should be in '.swell/skills/'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1.
Line 141: TrajectoryAnalyzer has #[allow(dead_code)] attribute and its store field is never used for database lookups. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1.
Line 955: Confidence calculation uses sum of all pattern frequencies (frequency_map.values().sum()) as denominator instead of per-pattern frequency. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1.

Line 1145: Convention parsing uses wrong string slice index [3..] instead of [4..]. For input '- **name**: description', position 0='-', 1=' ', 2='*', 3='*', 4='n'. Name starts at index 4. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1.
Line 1170: get_anti_patterns_for_error is an unimplemented stub returning empty vec with comment 'For MVP, return empty'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1.
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/pattern_learning.rs:1170-1172: Ok(Vec::new()) with 'For MVP, return empty' comment

blockingIssues.description: Lines 779, 795: get_by_type() and get_by_label() do not filter by repository, allowing cross-repository data leakage.
Line 330: Pagination bug - truncate(query.limit) is applied before skip(query.offset), producing incorrect result counts when offset > 0.
EventLog has log_observation() method missing despite ObservationPayload being defined. Feature description requires 'observation' as one of the immutable timestamped events.
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/event_log.rs:125 (ObservationPayload defined but no log_observation method)

blockingIssues.description: Line 662: find_best_models SQL orders by success_count instead of composite_score. A model with more raw successes but terrible efficiency/speed would rank higher.
Line 56: calculate_decay does not handle now < last_reinforcement. When num_days is negative, base.powf(negative) returns > 1.0, violating documented [0.0, 1.0] range.
Line 638: update() does not persist repository, language, or task_type fields. Lines 450, 462, 472: get_by_type/get_by_label/get_by_provenance don't filter by repository.
Lines 396, 608: delete() and get() don't validate repository scope - could access/modify memories from any repository by ID.
Line 681: analyze() returns hardcoded loss: 0.0 and fixed distances (0.5) instead of actual computed values. Line 668: analyze() lacks access to embeddings.
Line 595: BM25 search stream generates random UUIDs (Uuid::new_v4()) instead of actual memory entry IDs, making fusion results invalid.
Line 581: store() INSERT doesn't include provenance fields (source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context) - silently drops them. Line 631: update() also doesn't persist them.
Line 684: search() ignores source_episode_id filter in MemoryQuery despite the field existing for 'Optional source episode ID filter'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: CRITICAL SYSTEMIC ISSUE: 5 blocking issues have persisted for 21 rounds without resolution in memory-skill-extraction and memory-pattern-learning. These are well-identified, localized bugs with exact line numbers. The Rust skill procedure produces working code that passes tests but contains logic bugs that tests don't catch. Recommend: (1) Create a dedicated fix-memory-blocking-issues feature with explicit fix instructions for each issue, (2) Consider adding specific code review checklist for slice indices, denominators, and stub implementations that commonly have bugs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 5 issues marked 'STILL NOT FIXED since round 1' across 2 features. Tests pass but correctness bugs persist. Workers follow Rust skill correctly but miss these specific bugs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: MemoryStore trait (swell-core/src/traits.rs) defines get_by_type, get_by_label, get_by_provenance, get, delete without repository parameters, but scoping features require repository isolation. The trait design is inconsistent with the repository isolation requirement. Recommend: Update MemoryStore trait to include repository parameters for these methods.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-scoping, memory-sqlite-store, mem-provenance-tracking all report that the trait doesn't support repository filtering for these methods. Feature implementations can't fully enforce isolation without trait changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Event log uses io::Result<()> while other swell-memory modules use SwellError. AGENTS.md doesn't specify which error type to use, creating inconsistency across features. Recommend: Document the error handling convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-event-log-jsonl uses io::Result<()> but blocks.rs, recall.rs, semantic.rs use Result<T, SwellError>.
